stemHtml: Ground-Based Augmentation System (GBAS) provides differential GPS positioning to calculate an error correction. The ground-based receiver uses…

options.a: Only one satellite to check its position with the position calculated by the incoming signal, and then relays any error back to the satellite.
options.b: Timing signals from at least four satellites to establish the position error correction, which is then relayed to the aircraft's GPS receiver.
options.c: The precision codes broadcast by the satellites to determine any position errors.
options.d: Only the satellites being used by the aircraft to compute the error information.
